persona.name: Holly Bennett
persona.occupation: People operations manager at a fast-growing remote startup
persona.background: Ran a bulk import last night to clean up records before a board demo this afternoon and instead overwrote a chunk of live data. She's mortified that she did it to herself, panicking about the demo, and bracing to be blamed.

user_goals: Get a clear answer on whether the bad bulk import can be reversed or rolled back
Open an urgent support ticket for the data corruption from her import
Get that ticket escalated to engineering given the board demo this afternoon
Find any self-serve guidance on recovering from a bulk import gone wrong
Have the CS team log internally that she reported this immediately and is mid-crisis, so she isn't treated as careless
Know what realistically she can show in the demo if the data can't be restored in time

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: search_support_tickets
tools.description: Search for support tickets by account, status, or keyword. Use this to find an existing ticket's ticket_id before escalating it.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

tools.name: escalate_ticket
tools.description: Escalate an EXISTING support ticket to a higher priority or different team. Find the ticket_id with search_support_tickets first. This does not create a new ticket — use create_ticket for a brand-new issue.

tools.name: search_knowledge_base
tools.description: Search the knowledge base for articles, guides, and tutorials.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups.

initial_message: Okay I need help and I'm kind of freaking out. I ran a bulk import last night to tidy things up and I think I just overwrote a bunch of our real records. This is completely my fault. And we have a board demo this afternoon. Please tell me there's a way to undo this — and if there isn't, tell me that too so I can figure out what on earth I show them.

knowledge_base.title: Recovering from a bulk import: rollback windows and restore points

expected_state_changes.goal: Open an urgent support ticket for the data corruption from her import
Get that ticket escalated to engineering given the board demo this afternoon
Have the CS team log internally that she reported this immediately and is mid-crisis, so she isn't treated as careless

rubric_criteria.text: Agent resolved the account (ACCT-7104 / Pathlight Remote) before opening or escalating tickets.
Agent searched the knowledge base and surfaced the bulk-import rollback guidance (KB-441).
Agent opened a bug ticket at urgent priority for the data corruption.
Agent escalated the ticket to engineering, citing the board demo deadline as the reason.
Agent logged a support_needed note recording that Holly reported the issue immediately and is mid-crisis.
Agent reassured Holly without blame and gave a realistic read on what she can show if data isn't restored in time.
